rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic oxidative hydrofunctionalization of alkenes (hydroalkoxylation, hydroacyloxylation, hydroarylation, semipinacol rearrangement, deallylation) via a CoSalen-mediated Co(II/III/IV) HAT cycle.
electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes Co(II) to Co(III) (and later alkyl-Co(III) to alkyl-Co(IV)); cathode reduces MeOH to H2 and methoxide, which activates the silane to generate Co(III)-H for HAT to the alkene.
electron_flow: Anodic oxidation of Co(II) gives Co(III), which is converted to Co(III)-H by silane/methoxide; Co-H performs HAT to the alkene to give a C-radical that is trapped by Co(II) to form an alkyl-Co(III); a second anodic oxidation gives an electrophilic alkyl-Co(IV) that is intercepted by a nucleophile (alcohol, carboxylate, arene) near the anode, releasing product and regenerating Co(II).

probe_reagents.name: CD3OD solvent labeling
probe_reagents.observation: Very low D incorporation in product (<2% D) when CD3OD used vs Ph2SiD2 (70-80% D), confirming hydride comes from silane and indicating H/D scrambling via reversible HAT.

probe_reagents.name: aryl cyclopropane radical clock (14)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened trapping product (15) observed in 40% yield, supporting a discrete C-radical intermediate from HAT.

probe_reagents.name: Chiral CoSalen (3b, 3d)
probe_reagents.observation: Catalyst-controlled enantioselectivity (8% ee with 3b; -31% ee with 3d; up to -20% ee with RVC anode) indicates C-Nu bond formation occurs at a Co-bound intermediate (Co(IV)) rather than a free cation.

probe_reagents.name: Electrolyte variation (n-Bu4NBF4 vs LiClO4)
probe_reagents.observation: More hydrophobic NBu4BF4 gives lower methanol-trapping selectivity (1.9:1) vs LiClO4 (>20:1), consistent with Co(IV) trapping near the electrode within the EDL.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 3a
probe_reagents.observation: Reversible oxidation at E1/2 = -0.17 V vs Fc+/Fc; reduction wave disappears upon addition of KOMe, indicating methoxide is needed for silane activation/Co-H formation.

notes: Authors favor an ECEC mechanism with a discrete cationic Co(IV)-alkyl (24) trapped by nucleophile near the anode. Catalyst-controlled stereoselectivity and electrolyte (EDL) effects rule out a free carbocation diffusing into bulk. Side pathways include alkene isomerization and hydrogenation, attributed to back-HAT from the alkyl radical to Co(II) or deprotonation of Co(IV) complex 24. 1,1-disubstituted alkenes give predominantly isomerization, ascribed to disfavored Co(IV) formation.
